title: Cyathea hypolampra
taxonomy: Cyathea hypolampra is classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The genus Cyathea belongs to the family Cyatheaceae, commonly known as the tree fern family. This species is specifically placed within the genus due to its unique characteristics, such as its large, tree-like habit and distinctive leaf shape. Cyathea hypolampra is native to New Zealand and is an endemic species within the region. The classification of Cyathea hypolampra reflects its distinct evolutionary history and relationship to other ferns within the Cyatheaceae family.

intro: Cyathea hypolampra, commonly known as the Black Tree Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Cyathea genus and is native to Australia. The Black Tree Fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections.
